fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Georgia Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Georgia - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity georgia, how to transfer physical therapist license to georgia, physical therapist compact georgia, georgia physical therapist endorsement application

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in Georgia.

steps.title: Graduate from an Accredited Program & Request Transcripts
steps.description: Successfully complete a physical therapy program from a CAPTE-accredited institution. Arrange for official transcripts to be sent directly from your educational institution to the Georgia State Board of Physical Therapy.

steps.title: Create Account and Apply Online
steps.description: Create an account on the **Georgia Online Licensing System** (secure.sos.state.ga.us/myverification/) and complete the Physical Therapist initial licensure application. Pay the required application fee online.

steps.title: Register for and Pass the Georgia Jurisprudence Exam
steps.description: Register for and pass the Georgia Physical Therapy Jurisprudence Examination. This exam is typically administered online and must be passed to be eligible for licensure.

steps.title: Register for and Pass the National Physical Therapy Examination (NPTE)
steps.description: Register with the Federation of State Boards of Physical Therapy (FSBPT) for the National Physical Therapy Examination (NPTE) and pass it. Ensure your scores are sent directly to the Georgia State Board of Physical Therapy.

steps.title: Fulfill Other Requirements & Await Approval
steps.description: Ensure all supporting documentation (e.g., transcripts, NPTE scores, Jurisprudence exam results) has been received by the Board. The Board will review your complete application and notify you of the decision.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Georgia physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: For endorsement applicants, the state licensing fee is $75. Additional third-party costs (fingerprinting, transcripts, or verification) may apply.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Georgia have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Georgia participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Georgia without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Georgia?
faqs.answer: Current board processing time is 4 to 6 Weeks once your file is complete.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Georgia while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in Georgia
hero.dek: To apply for a Physical Therapist license in Georgia, begin by creating an account and completing the online application through the **Georgia Online Licensing System** (MyVerification portal).

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity

variants.label: Renewal

official_links.registration_steps: Visit the Georgia Online Licensing System, click 'Apply for a New License,' select 'Physical Therapist' from the dropdown, and create a new user account with your email address and password to begin the application.
